intent: Canonical single-permission file-read denial (logrotate_t reading a mislabeled shadow_t file). The anchor scenario - byte-identical in format to the f4 section 1.2 real captured AVC. Maps to one clean narrow allow.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic).

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: omits the auth_read_shadow() interface macro, which expands to: typeattribute logrotate_t can_read_shadow_passwords (policy-wide attribute), allow logrotate_t shadow_t:file read_file_perms (5 perms open/getattr/read/ioctl/lock vs the 1 denied), and allow logrotate_t etc_t:dir list_dir_perms (6 perms on the unrelated etc_t type that never appeared in a denial). Our expected.te emits only `allow logrotate_t shadow_t:file read;` - exactly the 1 denied perm, no macro, no attribute, no padding, no unrelated type.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl that mirrors audit2allow -R (the tool default) would emit the auth_read_shadow() macro and silently grant 5+ perms on shadow_t and 6 on etc_t plus a policy-wide attribute, instead of the single denied read perm.
